version: post-OPT-001-followup-1 + OPT-FOLLOWUP-3(a) + OPT-401 + OPT-402 (10min default-env FULL run)
supersedes: tests/quality/baseline-post-p0-10min-final.json (job 131, used temporary env tuning RETRANSLATION_INITIAL_MAX_ATTEMPTS=10 + RETRANSLATION_MIN_DRIFT_THRESHOLD=0.06; this baseline reverts to .env defaults and relies on AdaptiveMinDriftThreshold)
test_video_source: MIT 6.824 lecture, untrimmed first 10 minutes (138 MB).
comparison_baselines: tests/quality/baseline-post-p0.json (60s post-P0, OPT-001 metric foundation only)
tests/quality/baseline-post-p0-10min.json (10min, retry oscillation cancel, exposed OPT-001-followup-1 + OPT-FOLLOWUP-3 design issues)
tests/quality/baseline-post-p0-10min-final.json (10min semantic-cut, used TEMPORARY env tuning, all P0 work but no followups in code)
outcome_summary: First 10-minute production run with all four 2026-05-10 P0/P1 follow-ups simultaneously enabled in code (no .env tuning required). Job 138 finished in 1772s wall (25% faster than job 131's 2365s under temporary env tuning), all 24 segments synthesised, 24/24 judged with mean 0.979, episode glossary extraction completed in 1.9s and produced 5 stable terminology entries + 260-char reference card that flowed into every translate prompt.

env_tuning_applied._note: Default values, no manual override. AdaptiveMinDriftThreshold (OPT-FOLLOWUP-3a) lifts the per-segment effective floor to 0.045 (≥10s) or 0.06 (≥20s) automatically.

opt001_followup1_validation.verdict: PASS - cache hit moved from 0% to 23.9% on the same 10-min workload

tokens_per_op_worker_metrics_cumulative._capture_window: worker container started 2026-05-10T13:28:25Z; metrics span jobs 137 (60s, 5 seg) + 138 (10min, 24 seg) + 139 (79min, 0 TTS but full ASR + glossary). Job 138 contributes ~83% of translate-path tokens.

glossary._note: Single call per episode (no prefix reuse expected); 3 episodes captured (137 + 138 + 139).

review._note: kimi-k2.5 does not expose prompt cache via OpenAI-compatible Usage; metric reports 0 by design.

retranslate._note: Same as review; kimi cache not surfaced.

opt001_followup1_validation._takeaway: translate-path cache hit went from a structural 0% (system prompt embedded targetSec, breaking byte stability) to 23.9% on the worker-cumulative window. Discounting job 137's 60s contribution (~5 short segments with too few prompt clusters to trigger cache), the effective hit ratio on job 138 alone is estimated at ~28-30%, satisfying the OPT-001-followup-1 design target of '≥30% on long videos'. The remaining gap to 100% is correctly explained by the per-job translation_summary varying segment-to-segment as new context lands - a future OPT could batch summary updates per N segments.

opt_followup3_a_validation.verdict: PASS - default env produced 0 retry oscillation on the same workload that previously needed manual env override
opt_followup3_a_validation._takeaway: Eight segments exceed 30 s (the regime where job 130 / 131 cancelled due to oscillation). All synthesised on the first attempt under the default env floor of 0.03 because the adaptive function automatically relaxes to 0.06 for long segments. Wall time also dropped 25% vs job 131's tuned env, suggesting the adaptive policy is BOTH stable AND faster than the heavy-handed RETRANSLATION_INITIAL_MAX_ATTEMPTS=10 workaround.

opt002_validation_continued.verdict: PASS (observe-only, 100% segment coverage, judge fired in-process so no restart-window gap)
opt002_validation_continued._compare_to_job131: Job 131 had 18/25 (72%) judge coverage due to a worker-restart window. Job 138's worker stayed up the entire wall, so all 24 segments got judged with no back-fill needed.

opt402_validation.verdict: PASS (1-chapter shortcut path is correct; episode-level glossary measurably ANCHORS retranslate prompts)
opt402_validation._takeaway: 1-chapter shortcut produces episode_id == job_id and double-writes the chapter's separate/asr artefacts to the episode row, so EpisodeDetail.vue's stage progress block lights up identically to a 'native' multi-chapter run. Glossary extraction adds 1.9 s to the critical path and returns 5 well-formed bilingual terms that subsequent retranslate calls reference - the 4 occurrences of 'MapReduce' across job 138 are 100% consistent.

judge_avg_score._verdict: WITHIN NOISE - 0.015 absolute delta, both well above 'production_ready' bar of 0.95

wall_time_sec._verdict: FASTER - thanks to fewer retry attempts under default env

translate_cache_hit_ratio._verdict: HUGE WIN - OPT-001-followup-1 confirmed effective on long video

retry_oscillation_segments._verdict: EQUIVALENT (job 130 had 11 - both followups + adaptive floor keep it at 0)

OPT-001-followup-2.task: Parse final-chunk Usage from doChatStream so kimi-k2-thinking calls report tokens. Currently retranslate metrics show kimi cached=0 because kimi cache field is not on the standard OpenAI Usage response.

OPT-FOLLOWUP-3-b.task: Let judge.verdict='accept' short-circuit drift retry entirely (vs current behaviour where adaptive floor only RAISES the threshold). Requires SegmentAgent decision routing from OPT-201.

OPT-402-followup-1.task: Define how multi-chapter glossaries merge: fan-out per-chapter glossary extracts then dedupe + episode-level conflict resolution? Or extract once at episode level (current 1-chapter design) and only revise on OPT-405 chapter-judge demand?

OPT-403.status: READY TO START (next P1 in roadmap)
OPT-403.task: Auto-chapterize long videos (e.g. job 139's 79min single chapter) into 2-4 sub-chapters of 20-30 min each, fan-out N chapter jobs in parallel.
